The M621 Motorway (Junction 7) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The National Assistance (Sums for Personal Requirements) Amendment (England) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Assistance (Sums for Personal Requirements) (England) Regulations 2003 so that the weekly sum which local authorities in England are to assume, in the absence of special circumstances, that residents in accommodation arranged under Part 3 of the National Assistance Act 1948 will need for their personal requirements is increased to £23.50.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating Contributions (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2012
items.description: Under Part 2 of Schedule 8 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (“the 1988 Act”), billing authorities are required to pay amounts (called non-domestic rating contributions) to an account held by the Secretary of State (“the national non-domestic rating pool”). The national non-domestic rating pool is subsequently distributed to authorities under the rules in Part 3 of Schedule 8 to the 1988 Act. Payments into the national non-domestic rating pool in respect of the provisional amount of the non-domestic rating contributions are made during the financial year. Final calculations and adjustments of those contributions are made after the year ends.

items.title: The A1 Trunk Road (Thornhaugh, Peterborough) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Localism Act 2011 (Housing and Regeneration Functions in Greater London) (Consequential, Transitory, Transitional and Saving Provisions) Order 2012
items.description: Section 191(1) of the Localism Act 2011 (“the Act”) abolishes the London Development Agency (“the Agency”) from the date section 191(1) is brought into force, namely, 31st March 2012. The Act also makes amendments to, and repeals, primary legislation in consequence of the Agency’s abolition.

items.title: The St Albans and Welwyn Hatfield (Boundary Change) Order 2012
items.description: This Order gives effect to a recommendation by the Local Government Boundary Commission for England for the making of a change to the boundary between the borough of Welwyn Hatfield and the city of St Albans in the county of Hertfordshire.

items.title: The Standards Board for England (Abolition) Order 2012
items.description: This Order makes provision in connection with the abolition of the Standards Board for England (‘the Board’) on the abolition date of 1st April 2012.

items.title: The Armed Forces Act 2011 (Commencement No. 1, Transitional and Transitory Provisions) Order 2012
items.description: This Order brings into force certain provisions of the Armed Forces Act 2011 (c. 18). The provisions listed in article 3 are brought into force on 8th March 2012 (though in some cases for limited purposes only), and those listed in article 4 on 2nd April 2012. Articles 6 to 12 make transitional provision in relation to some of the provisions listed in article 4. Article 13 is a transitory provision.

items.title: The Access to the Countryside (Appeals against Works Notices) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend the Access to the Countryside (Appeals against Works Notices) (England) Regulations 2011 (S.I. 2011/2019), which came into force on 1st October 2011; they correct certain minor drafting defects in, and make certain minor clarifications to, those earlier Regulations.

items.title: The Personal Injuries (Civilians) Scheme (Amendment) Order 2012
items.description: This Order further amends the Personal Injuries (Civilians) Scheme 1983 (“the principal Scheme”) which makes provision for the payment of pensions and allowances to or in respect of civilians who were killed or injured during the 1939-1945 World War.

items.title: The Feed-in Tariffs (Specified Maximum Capacity and Functions) (Amendment) Order 2012 (revoked)
items.description: This Order amends the Feed-in Tariffs (Specified Maximum Capacity and Functions) Order 2010 (S.I. 2010/678) (“the 2010 Order”).

items.title: The Council Tax (Administration and Enforcement) (Amendment) (England) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend the Council Tax (Administration and Enforcement) Regulations 1992 by introducing a new regulation 21A and 21B into those Regulations. The amendment is consequential on the introduction, under the Localism Act 2011, of council tax referendums in England.

items.title: The Teachers’ Pensions (Amendment) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st April 2012, make amendments to the Teachers’ Pensions Regulations 2010.

items.title: The Consistent Financial Reporting (England) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Consistent Financial Reporting (England) Regulations 2003 and several sets of amending Regulations.

items.title: The Tobacco Advertising and Promotion (Display and Specialist Tobacconists) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend the Tobacco Advertising and Promotion (Display) (England) Regulations 2010 (the “Display Regulations”) and the Tobacco Advertising and Promotion (Specialist Tobacconists) (England) Regulations 2010 (the “Specialist Tobacconist Regulations”).

items.title: The Family Procedure (Amendment) Rules 2012
items.description: These Rules amend the Family Procedure Rules 2010 (“the FPR”).

items.title: The Welfare Reform Act 2009 (Commencement No. 7) Order 2012
items.description: This Order brings into force section 29 of the Welfare Reform Act 2009 on 19th January 2012 for the purpose of conferring power to make Regulations under paragraph 8B of Schedule 1 to the Jobseekers Act 1995 (‘the Act’) and for the purpose of prescribing the Parliamentary procedure that applies in relation to regulations made under paragraph 8B of Schedule 1 to the Act.

items.title: The Police (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Regulations 2003 in a number of respects with regard to testing for the consumption of controlled drugs or alcohol. The amendments allow for the testing of any member of a police force as part of a routine random testing regime, rather than the testing of members in particular categories or particular circumstances. They allow a sample of hair, rather than oral fluid or urine, to be taken for the purposes of testing a candidate for appointment to a police force. They also replace references to saliva in the Police Regulations 2003 with references to oral fluid, while defining oral fluid so as to include saliva. This last is a technical amendment to reflect the fact that the substance recovered by oral testing is a fluid containing, but not consisting entirely of, saliva.

items.title: The Closure of Prisons (H.M. Prison Latchmere House) Order 2012
items.description: This Order provides for the closure of H.M. Prison Latchmere House on 1st April 2012.

items.title: The Pensions Act 2011 (Commencement No. 2) Order 2012
items.description: This Order brings into force sections 9, 12 and 13 of the Pensions Act 2011 (c. 19) in so far as they are not already in force, on 6th March 2012.

items.title: The Pensions Act 2008 (Commencement No. 12) Order 2012
items.description: This Order brings fully into force sections 14, 15A and 28 of the Pensions Act 2008 (c. 30) (“the Act”) as amended by the Pensions Act 2011 (c. 19) (“the 2011 Act”).

items.title: The Postal Services Act 2011 (Transfer of Accrued Pension Rights) Order 2012
items.description: This Order implements sections 17, 18, 19 and 20 of the Postal Services Act 2011 (c. 5) (“the Act”).

items.title: The Postal Services Act 2011 (Transfer of Assets) Order 2012
items.description: This Order implements sections 21 and 22 of the Postal Services Act 2011 (c. 5) (“the Act”). It transfers assets from the Royal Mail Pension Plan (“RMPP”) to a Fund established by the Secretary of State for Business, Innovation and Skills (the Business Innovation and Skills Fund Limited, “the Fund”) and prescribes how the assets and liabilities of the RMPP must be valued for the purposes of the transfer. This Order will come into force at the same time as the Postal Services Act 2011 (Transfer of Accrued Pension Rights) Order 2012 (SI No. 2012/687) (“the Benefits Order”), which transfers all the historic liabilities of the RMPP to a new public scheme.

items.title: The Taxes, etc. (Fees for Payment by Telephone) Regulations 2012 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which replace the Taxes, etc. (Fees for Payment by Telephone) Regulations 2009 (S.I. 2009/3073), provide that a fee must be paid in respect of payments by credit card made over the telephone equal to 1.5% of the payment with effect from 2nd April 2012 (it was 1.25% of the payment under the 2009 Regulations).

items.title: The A1 Trunk Road (Leeming to Catterick) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Authorised Officers (Meat Inspection) (Revocation) (England) Regulations 2012
The Pensions Act 2004 (Disclosure of Restricted Information by the Pensions Regulator – Amendment) Order 2012
items.description: This Order amends Schedule 3 to the Pensions Act 2004 (c.35) to replace references to the Institute of Actuaries and to the Faculty of Actuaries with references to the Institute and Faculty of Actuaries, which is the new body formed by the merger of the Institute of Actuaries and the Faculty of Actuaries.

items.title: The Pensions (Institute and Faculty of Actuaries and Consultation by Employers – Amendment) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend 15 Statutory Instruments and one Northern Ireland Statutory Rule about occupational pension schemes, the Pension Protection Fund, the Pensions Regulator and pension sharing.

items.title: The Guaranteed Minimum Pensions Increase Order 2012
items.description: This Order specifies 3 per cent. as the percentage by which that part of any guaranteed minimum pension attributable to earnings factors for the tax years 1988-89 to 1996-97 and payable by contracted-out, defined benefit occupational pension schemes is to be increased. Under section 109(3) of the Pension Schemes Act 1993 (c.48) the percentage to be specified is the actual percentage increase in the general level of prices in the period under review or 3 per cent., whichever is less.

items.title: The Education (School Teachers’ Prescribed Qualifications, etc) (Amendment) Order 2012
items.description: This Order amends the Education (School Teachers’ Prescribed Qualifications, etc) Order 2003 with effect from 1st April 2012.

items.title: The Flexible Tenancies (Review Procedures) Regulations 2012
items.description: Part IV of the Housing Act 1985 provides for a regime of flexible tenancies. If the landlord offers to grant a flexible tenancy and the length of the term does not, in the opinion of the prospective tenant, accord with the policy of the landlord as to the length of terms of the flexible tenancies it grants, the prospective tenant may request a review. These Regulations make provision about the procedure to be followed in conducting such reviews.

items.title: The Transfer of Tenancies and Right to Acquire (Exclusion) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations set out descriptions of assured shorthold tenancies that are excluded from tenancy transfer under section 158 of the Localism Act 2011 and the right to acquire under sections 180 to 185 of the Housing and Regeneration Act 2008.

items.title: The Plant Health (Miscellaneous Amendments) (England) Regulations 2012 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to plant health legislation applying in England.

items.title: The Marine and Coastal Access Act 2009 (Transitional Provisions) Order 2012
items.description: This Order extends the transitional provisions in paragraph 9 of Schedule 9 to the Marine and Coastal Access Act 2009 (“the 2009 Act”) for certain dredging operations. This Order applies in relation to dredging operations which are licensable marine activities for the purposes of Part 4 of the 2009 Act, other than those for which the appropriate licensing authority is the Welsh Ministers or the Department of the Environment in Northern Ireland, and subject to certain further exceptions.

items.title: The M6 Motorway (Junctions 39-41 Northbound and Southbound Carriageways) (Temporary Restriction of Traffic) Order 2012
The Housing (Scotland) Act 2010 (Consequential Provisions and Modifications) Order 2012
items.description: This Order makes provision in consequence of the Housing (Scotland) Act 2010 (asp 17) (“the 2010 Act”). The 2010 Act creates a body corporate to be known as the Scottish Housing Regulator and makes provision about the performance and regulation of social landlords in Scotland.

items.title: The London Legacy Development Corporation (Tax Consequences) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations deal with tax consequences of transfers of property, rights and liabilities from the Olympic Park Legacy Company Limited (“OPLC”) to the London Legacy Development Corporation (“LLDC”) made under the London Legacy Development Corporation and the Olympic Park Legacy Company Transfer Scheme 2012 (“the OPLC Scheme”). They also deal with tax consequences of transfers of trading stock from the London Thames Gateway Development Corporation (“LTGDC”) to LLDC under the London Legacy Development Corporation and the London Thames Gateway Development Corporation (No. 1) Transfer Scheme 2012 (“the LTGDC Scheme”).

items.title: The Localism Act 2011 (Housing and Regeneration Functions in Greater London) (Consequential, Transitional and Saving Provisions) (No. 2) Order 2012
items.description: Section 189 of the Localism Act 2011 (“the Act”), which is brought into force on 1st April 2012 (“the commencement date”), introduces a definition of England in respect of the Homes and Communities Agency’s objects which excludes Greater London. It also makes other amendments to Part 1 of the Housing and Regeneration Act 2008 to exclude Greater London from references to England. The purpose of these changes is to facilitate the transfer of the functions of the Agency, in so far as they relate to Greater London, to the Greater London Authority (“the Authority”).

items.title: The M3 Motorway (Junction 2, Westbound Carriageway) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The M25 Motorway and the A282 Trunk Road (Junctions 30 - 31) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Individual Savings Account (Amendment) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 6th April 2012, amend the Individual Savings Account Regulations 1998 (S.I. 1998/1870) (“the ISA Regulations”) by increasing the overall subscription limit for ISA accounts other than a junior ISA.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Northampton Sywell) Regulations 2012
The M25 Motorway and the A30 Trunk Road (M25 Junction 13) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Wimbledon) Regulations 2012
The Pensions Act 2008 (Abolition of Protected Rights) (Consequential Amendments) (No.2) (Amendment) Order 2012
items.description: This Order is made under section 145 of the Pensions Act 2008 (c.30), which provides for amendments to be made in consequence of section 106 of that Act. Section 106 provides that pension schemes contracted-out on a defined contributions basis are not required to make special provision in relation to the protected rights of members, from the contracting-out abolition date (the date of the coming into force of section 15(1) of the Pensions Act 2007 (c.22)).

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Jet Formation Display Teams) Regulations 2012
The Competition Act 1998 (Public Policy Exclusion) Order 2012
items.description: This Order excludes from the Chapter I prohibition in the Competition Act 1998 an agreement between the Secretary of State for Energy and Climate Change and representatives of the petroleum supply industry concerning the supply of fuel in an emergency. It also excludes from the prohibition certain arrangements implementing that agreement. The Chapter I prohibition precludes agreements between undertakings, decisions by associations of undertakings or concerted practices which may affect trade within the United Kingdom and which have as their object or effect the prevention, restriction or distortion of competition within the United Kingdom. Where there are exceptional and compelling reasons of public policy for doing so, the Secretary of State may exclude an agreement or an agreement of a particular description from the Chapter I prohibition.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Capital Finance and Accounting) (England) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 2012
items.description: The Regulations amend the Local Authorities (Capital Finance and Accounting) (England) Regulations 2003 (“the 2003 Regulations”).

items.title: The Child Support (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations are made under powers in the Child Support Act 1991 (c.48) (“the 1991 Act”) and come into force on 30th April 2012. They amend the Child Support (Collection and Enforcement) Regulations 1992 (“the Collection and Enforcement Regulations”), the Child Support (Maintenance Assessment Procedure) Regulations 1992 (“the Maintenance Assessment Procedure Regulations”), the Child Support (Maintenance Assessment and Special Cases) Regulations 1992 (“the Maintenance Assessment and Special Cases Regulations”), the Child Support (Maintenance Calculations and Special Cases) Regulations 2000 (“the Maintenance Calculations and Special Cases Regulations”) and the Child Support (Management of Payments and Arrears) Regulations 2009 (“the Management of Payments and Arrears Regulations”).

items.title: The M20 Motorway (Junctions 4 - 8) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The M1 Motorway (Junctions 1 - 4, Slip Roads) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The A40 Trunk Road (Denham Court Drive, Denham) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Humber Bridge (Debts) Order 2012
items.description: This Order releases the Humber Bridge Board from its obligation to pay the Secretary of State for Transport a proportion of principal and interest arising under agreements for the financing of the construction and maintenance of the Humber Bridge. This Order comes into force on 31st March 2012, save for article 2(3)(c), article 2(1) (to the extent that it relates to article 2(3)(c)) and article 3 which come into force on 1st April 2012.

items.title: The Government Resources and Accounts Act 2000 (Estimates and Accounts) Order 2012
items.description: This Order designates specified central government bodies in relation to named government departments for the purpose of those departments’ supply estimates and resource accounts.

items.title: The Building (Amendment) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend the Building Regulations 2010 (“the 2010 Regulations”).

items.title: The M20 Motorway and the A20 Trunk Road (Junctions 11a - 13, Slip Roads) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Cheltenham Festival) Regulations 2012
The M1 Motorway (Junctions 8 and 9) Slip Roads (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The A66 Trunk Road (Rheged to Brougham) (Temporary Prohibition and Restriction of Traffic) Order 2012
The A47 Trunk Road (Dereham, Norfolk) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The A27 Trunk Road (Warblington Interchange - Broad Marsh/Bedhampton Interchange, Carriageways) (Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2012
The Budget Responsibility and National Audit Act 2011 (Consequential Amendments) Order 2012
items.description: This Order makes amendments to subordinate legislation in consequence of the commencement of Part 2 of the Budget Responsibility and National Audit Act 2011 (c.4) (“the Act”). The amendments reflect the continuation of the office of Comptroller and Auditor General by section 11 of the Act and the establishment of the National Audit Office as a body corporate (no longer headed by the Comptroller and Auditor General) by section 20 of the Act.

items.title: The M65 Motorway (Junction 6, Eastbound Carriageway) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Budget Responsibility and National Audit Act 2011 (Appointed Day) Order 2012
items.description: This Order appoints 1st April 2012 as the appointed day for the purpose of section 25(2)(a) of the Budget Responsibility and National Audit Act 2011 (c.4.) (“the Act”), with the effect that the period which begins with that date and ends with 31st March 2013 is the first financial year for the purposes of Part 2 of the Act (National Audit).

items.title: The A303 Trunk Road (Picket Twenty Interchange - Salisbury Road Interchange) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Royal International Air Tattoo RAF Fairford) Regulations 2012
The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Silverstone and Turweston) Regulations 2012
The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Royal Air Force Cosford) Regulations 2012
The M4 Motorway (Junctions 1 - 3) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The A27 Trunk Road (Holmbush Interchange to Hangleton) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The A46 Trunk Road (Evesham, Worcestershire) (Temporary Restriction of Traffic) Order 2012
The Housing (Right to Buy) (Limit on Discount) (England) Order 2012 (revoked)
items.description: A person exercising the right to buy a dwelling-house under Part V of the Housing Act 1985 may be entitled, under sections 129 to 131 of and Schedule 4 to the Act, to a discount equal to a percentage of the price before discount.

items.title: The Finance Act 2010, Schedule 6, Part 1 (Further Consequential and Incidental Provision etc) Order 2012
items.description: This Order comes into force on 1st April 2012 and makes further consequential and incidental provision in consequence of, or in connection with, Part 1 of Schedule 6 to the Finance Act 2010 (c. 13) (“Schedule 6”).

items.title: The Finance Act 2010, Schedule 6, Part 2 (Commencement) Order 2012
items.description: This Order applies the new definitions of “charity”, “charitable company” and “charitable trust” set out in Part 1 of Schedule 6 to the Finance Act 2010 (c.13) for the purposes of certain tax enactments. It does this by bringing into force amendments in Part 2 of Schedule 6 which remove existing definitions applying for the purposes of those enactments (see articles 3 to 22). On the coming into force of these amendments, the effect of paragraph 33(2) of Schedule 6 is that the new definitions will apply.

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Abingdon Air and Country Show) Regulations 2012
The A5 Trunk Road and M6 Toll Motorway (Churchbridge, Staffordshire) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Merchant Shipping (Ship-to-Ship Transfers) (Amendment) Regulations 2012 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Merchant Shipping (Ship-to-Ship Transfers) Regulations 2010 (“the 2010 Regulations”).

items.title: The M5 Motorway (Junction 5) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The A45 Trunk Road (Festival Roundabout, Coventry) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Plant Health (Fees) (England) Regulations 2012 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which apply in England only, specify fees payable to the Secretary of State in the field of plant health.

items.title: The Further Education Teachers’ Qualifications, Continuing Professional Development and Registration (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2012 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Further Education Teachers' Qualifications (England) Regulations 2007 (S.I. 2007/2264) and the Further Education Teachers' Continuing Professional Development and Registration (England) Regulations 2007 (S.I. 2007/2116).

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (General Permitted Development) (Amendment) (England) Order 2012 (revoked)
items.description: This Order amends Schedule 2 to the Town and Country Planning (General Permitted Development) Order 1995 (S.I. 1995/418) in relation to permitted development rights, in England, for the installation of certain microgeneration equipment. Where permitted development rights apply, no specific application for planning permission is required.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Compensation) (England) Regulations 2012 (revoked)
items.description: Section 108 of the Town and Country Planning Act 1990 provides for the payment of compensation in certain cases where planning permission for development granted by a development order or a local development order is withdrawn and where on an application for planning permission for that development, the application is refused or permission is granted subject to conditions.

items.title: The Police Reform and Social Responsibility Act 2011 (Commencement No. 3 and Transitional Provisions) (Amendment) Order 2012
items.description: This Order amends the Police Reform and Social Responsibility Act 2011 (Commencement No. 3 and Transitional Provisions) Order 2011 (“the 2011 Order”).

items.title: The Criminal Defence Service (Funding) (Amendment) Order 2012
items.description: This Order amends the Criminal Defence Service (Funding) Order 2007. It clarifies the fact that disbursements incurred by litigators in a magistrates’ court in a case subsequently committed or sent to the Crown Court are payable in accordance with the terms of the Order. It includes a preliminary hearing and a hearing relating to breach of bail, failure to surrender to bail or execution of a bench warrant within the definition of standard appearance.

items.title: The Measuring Instruments (EEC Requirements) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend the Measuring Instruments (EEC Requirements) (Fees) Regulations 2004 (“the 2004 Regulations”), which prescribe the fees to be charged in relation to certain services provided by the Secretary of State with regards to measuring instruments.

items.title: The A49 Trunk Road (South of Hereford) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012

The Imperial College Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Establishment) and the Hammersmith Hospitals National Health Service Trust and the St Mary’s National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) (Amendment) Order 2012
items.description: This Order amends the Imperial College Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Establishment) and the Hammersmith Hospitals National Health Service Trust and the St Mary’s National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2007 (the Establishment Order) which established the Imperial College Healthcare National Health Service Trust. Article 2 amends article 3 of the Establishment Order which set out the trust’s functions, in particular so that the restriction on only providing hospital accommodation and services and community health services is removed and the trust is now established to provide goods and services for the purposes of the health service.

items.title: The Reporting of Savings Income and Interest Payments (Data-gathering) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations make a consequential amendment and revocations following the repeal of Section 17 of the Taxes Management Act 1970 by paragraph 51 of Schedule 23 to the Finance Act 2011 (c. 11) (data-gathering powers) (“Schedule 23”). There is no power in Schedule 23 to make consequential amendments or revocations.

items.title: The Social Security (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend various sets of social security regulations.

items.title: The M1 Motorway (Between Junctions 20 and 21, Leicestershire) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The M1 Motorway (Junction 24a) (Northbound Entry Slip Road) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The A38 Trunk Road (Saltash Tunnel) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Fees)(Amendment) Order 2012
items.description: This Order amends the Births, Deaths and Marriages (Fees) Order 2010 by replacing the Schedule to that Order, which sets out the fees payable, with a new schedule that includes a number of amendments to the fees payable under the Acts relating to the registration of births, deaths and marriages and associated matters from 1 April 2012. Both the old and the new fees are set out in the Schedule.

items.title: The Registration of Civil Partnerships (Fees) (Amendment) Order 2012 (revoked)
items.description: This Order amends the Registration of Civil Partnerships (Fees) Order 2005 and the Registration of Civil Partnerships (Fees) (No. 2) Order 2005 by amending some of the fees payable in relation to the registration of civil partnerships from 1st April 2012.

items.title: The Education (Specified Work) (England) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Education (Specified Work and Registration) (England) Regulations 2003 (“the 2003 Regulations”) as amended. They come into force on 1st April 2012.

items.title: The Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Exemption) (Amendment) Order 2012
items.description: This Order amends the Schedule to the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Exemption) Order 2001 (S.I. 2001/1201) (“the principal order”), which provides for certain persons to be exempt from the general prohibition imposed by section 19 of the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (c.8) on carrying on a regulated activity in the United Kingdom unless authorised under that Act.

items.title: The Postal Services Act 2011 (Taxation) Regulations 2012
items.description: The Postal Services Act 2011 (c. 5) (“the Act”) makes provision for the transfer of the historic deficit of the Royal Mail Pension Plan (the “RMPP”) to the government. Part 2 of the Act provides powers for the Secretary of State to establish a new statutory pension scheme (“the new public scheme”), transfer rights from the RMPP to the new public scheme, remove liabilities from the RMPP and transfer assets from the RMPP to the government. These Regulations make provision in relation to the taxation consequences of the restructuring of the RMPP and the new public scheme in relation to accrued rights of members of the RMPP and the transfer of assets of the RMPP.

items.title: The Education Act 2011 (Consequential Amendments to Subordinate Legislation) Order 2012
items.description: This Order makes amendments to secondary legislation in consequence of the abolition of the Training and Development Agency for England, the General Teaching Council for England and the Qualifications and Curriculum Development Agency. In consequence of the amendments made by this Order a number of provisions in the Apprenticeships, Skills Children and Learning Act 2009, Parts 7 and 8 (Consequential Amendments) Order 2010 are also revoked.

items.title: The Social Security (Credits) (Amendment) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 9C of the 1975 Regulations to provide for earnings equal to the lower earnings limit to be credited in respect of any week during which a person was paid additional statutory paternity pay.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Local Planning) (England) Regulations 2012
items.description: Part 2 of the Planning and Compulsory Purchase Act 2004 (“the Act”) established a system of local development planning in England. These Regulations make provision for the operation of that system.

items.title: The M54 Motorway (Junction 2) (Westbound Entry Slip Road) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The A49 Trunk Road (Leominster to Ludlow) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The A38 Trunk Road (South Brent, Devon) (Temporary Prohibition and Restriction of Traffic) Order 2012
The A1 Trunk Road (Eighton Lodge Interchange) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Immigration (Passenger Transit Visa)(Amendment)(No. 2) Order 2012 (revoked)
items.description: This Order is made under section 41 of the Immigration and Asylum Act 1999. It amends the Immigration (Passenger Transit Visa) Order 2003 (the  “2003 Order”). The 2003 Order requires certain passengers to hold a transit visa to pass through the United Kingdom without entering whilst transiting to another country or territory.

items.title: The A1 Trunk Road (Fawdon Interchange to Kenton Bar Interchange) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The A19 Trunk Road (Tontine Interchange) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The A1 Trunk Road (Darrington Interchange) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The M180 Motorway (Junction 3 to Junction 2) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The M606 Motorway (Junction 1 to Junction 3) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The A1(M) Motorway (Junction 60 to Junction 61) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The National Health Service Trusts (Originating Capital) Order 2012
items.description: This Order determines the amount of the originating capital, in accordance with paragraph 1(1) of Schedule 5 to the National Health Service Act 2006, of National Health Service trusts established under that Act between 1st April 2011 and 1st September 2011.

items.title: The M5 Motorway (Junctions 18 and 18A) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Social Security Benefits Up-rating Order 2012 (revoked)
items.description: This Order is made as a consequence of a review under section 150 of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 (“the Administration Act”) and includes details of the sums mentioned in that section.

items.title: The M60 Motorway Junction 12 (Clockwise Link Road to the M62 Westbound Carriageway) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Pensions Increase (Review) Order 2012
items.description: Under section 59 of the Social Security Pensions Act 1975 as amended, and as modified by section 59A of that Act, the Treasury (in whom the functions conferred by those provisions are now vested) are required to provide by order for the increase in the rates of public service pensions. Under section 59(7) of the 1975 Act, section 59 of that Act has effect as if it were contained in the Pensions (Increase) Act 1971. Section 9(2)(c) of the 1971 Act provides that references to the “rate” of pension shall have effect as references also to the amount of a lump sum benefit. The increase is the percentage (or in some circumstances a fraction of the percentage) by which the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions has, by direction given under the provisions of section 151(1) of the Social Security Administration Act 1992, increased the sums referred to in section 150(1)(c) of that Act. These are the sums which are the additional pensions in long term benefits, namely the additional pension entitlements accruing to employees in respect of earnings after 5th April 1978.

items.title: The A5036 Trunk Road (Junctions with the A5038 Netherton Way and the B5194 Copy Lane, Litherland) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The M60 Motorway (Junctions 18-19), the M66 Motorway (Junction 4) and the M62 Motorway (Junction 18) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Traffic Management (St Helens Borough Council) Permit Scheme Order 2012
items.description: This Order gives effect to the St Helens Borough Council Permit Scheme.

items.title: The Isle of Wight National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 2012
items.description: This Order establishes a National Health Service trust called the Isle of Wight National Health Service Trust.

items.title: The Infrastructure Planning (Environmental Impact Assessment) (Amendment) Regulations 2012 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Infrastructure Planning (Environmental Impact Assessment) Regulations 2009  (“the 2009 Regulations”). The 2009 Regulations implemented, in relation to nationally significant infrastructure projects, the Directive on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private projects on the environment.

items.title: The Torbay and Southern Devon Health and Care National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 2012
items.description: This Order establishes a National Health Service trust called the Torbay and Southern Devon Health and Care National Health Service Trust.

items.title: The M4 Motorway (Junction 19) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Balado) Regulations 2012
The Qualifying Care Relief (Specified Social Care Schemes) (Amendment) Order 2012
items.description: This Order amends the Qualifying Care Relief (Specified Social Care Schemes) Order 2011 (S.I. 2011/712) (“the 2011 Order”).

items.title: The National Savings Bank (Investment Deposits) (Limits) (Amendment) Order 2012 (revoked)
items.description: This Order amends the National Savings Bank (Investment Deposits) (Limits) Order 1977 (S.I. 1977/1210) by increasing, from £5,340 to £5,640, the annual amount which can be accepted by the Director of Savings from any person by way of deposit in an individual savings account at the National Savings Bank.

items.title: The Barts Health National Health Service Trust (Establishment) and the Barts and The London National Health Service Trust, the Newham University Hospital National Health Service Trust and the Whipps Cross University Hospital National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2012
items.description: This Order establishes the Barts Health National Health Service Trust as a National Health Service trust.

items.title: The European Union (Definition of Treaties) (Second Agreement amending the Cotonou Agreement) Order 2012 (revoked)
items.description: This Order declares the Agreement amending for the second time the 2000 Partnership Agreement between the Members of the African, Caribbean and Pacific Group of States, of the one part, and the European Community and its Member States, of the other part (the Cotonou Agreement), signed in Ouagadougou, Burkina Faso on the 22nd of June 2010, to be an EU Treaty as defined in section 1(2) of the European Communities Act 1972.

items.title: The Consular Fees Order 2012
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces the Consular Fees Order 2011 as amended by the Consular Fees (Amendment) Order 2011.

items.title: The Copyright and Performances (Application to Other Countries) Order 2012 (revoked)
items.description: Part I of the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 (“the Act”) confers copyright on the creators of certain works. Part II of the Act confers rights on performers and persons having recording rights in relation to a performance. The purpose of this Order is to apply Part I to works originating from other countries and to confer on certain countries reciprocal protection under Part II. This Order provides a consolidated list of countries in relation to which provisions of the Act apply. In this Order the term  “country” includes any territory, by virtue of sections 178 and 211 of the Act.

items.title: The School Admissions (Admission Arrangements and Co-ordination of Admission Arrangements) (England) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations are made under sections 88B, 88C, 88E, 88F, 88H, 88K, 88M, 88N, 88O, 88Q, 92, 100, 102, and 138(7) of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998 (“SSFA 1998”), and section 29(5) of the Education Act 1996, and come into force on 1st February 2012 primarily in relation to admission arrangements for maintained schools for the academic year 2013-2014 and subsequent years (but see chapter 6 below and Part 3 below).

items.title: The A419 Trunk Road (Blunsdon to Calcutt, Wiltshire) (Prohibition of U Turns) (Experimental) Order 2012
The Trafford Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Dissolution) Order 2012
items.description: This Order provides for the dissolution on 1st April 2012 of the Trafford Healthcare National Health Service Trust established on 8th February 1994 and revokes the Trafford Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1994 that established the trust.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Limits and Thresholds) (Amendment) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 2001 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Re-rating) Order 2012
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on 6th April 2012, increases the rates of Class 2 and Class 3 contributions specified in sections 11(1) and 13(1) of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 (“the Act”) from £2.50 to £2.65 and from £12.60 to £13.25. It also increases the amount of earnings specified in section 11(4) of the Act, below which an earner may be excepted from liability for Class 2 contributions from £5,315 to £5,595 (Articles 2 and 3).

items.title: The Ministry of Defence Police (Performance) Regulations 2012 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations establish procedures in respect of unsatisfactory performance or attendance of members of the Ministry of Defence Police force of the rank of chief superintendent or below (excluding probationers in the first year). They apply in relation to unsatisfactory performance or attendance that comes to the attention of the officer’s line manager on or after 6th April 2012.

items.title: The Energy Performance of Buildings (Certificates and Inspections)(England and Wales)(Amendment) Regulations 2012 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Energy Performance of Buildings (Certificates and Inspections)(England and Wales) Regulations 2007 (“the 2007 Regulations”).

items.title: The A1 Trunk Road (North Charlton) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Export Control (Syria Sanctions) and (Miscellaneous Amendments) Order 2012 (revoked)
items.description: This Order makes provision for the enforcement of certain restrictive measures specified in Council Regulation (EU) No 36/2012 (OJ L 16, 19.1.2012, p1), as last amended by Council Regulation (EU) No 168/2012, concerning restrictive measures in view of the situation in Syria and repealing Council Regulation (EU) 442/2011 (“the Syria Regulation”).

items.title: The Controlled Waste (England and Wales) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations classify waste as household waste, industrial waste or commercial waste for the purposes of Part 2 of the Environmental Protection Act 1990 (“the Act”) and in consequence determine the meaning of “controlled waste” for the purposes of Part 2 of the Act (the definition of “controlled waste” is in section 75(4)).

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Kemble) Regulations 2012
The Immigration and Nationality (Cost Recovery Fees) Regulations 2012 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, made under sections 51(3), 52(1) and 52(3) of the Immigration, Asylum and Nationality Act 2006, specify fees relating to immigration and nationality. They are made for the purposes of the Immigration and Nationality (Fees) Order 2011 (S.I. 2011/445), which sets out the applications, services and processes related to immigration and nationality for which fees may be specified in regulations.

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Authorised Fuels) (England) Regulations 2012 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations consolidate six sets of regulations which declare fuels to be authorised fuels for the purposes of Part III of the Clean Air Act 1993. Those regulations are SI 2008/514, SI 2008/2342, SI 2009/2191, SI 2010/576, SI 2011/715 and SI 2011/2105. Two additional fuels are authorised (Excel+ briquettes and Newheat briquettes). Briteheat and Stoveheat Premium briquettes are two names for the same product and they are therefore listed together.

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Exempted Fireplaces) (England) Order 2012 (revoked)
items.description: This Order revokes and replaces with amendments the Smoke Control Areas (Exempted Fireplaces) (England) (No 2) Order 2011 (S.I. 2011/2106).

items.title: The Social Security (Categorisation of Earners) (Amendment) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Categorisation of Earners) Regulations 1978 (“the principal Regulations”) and the Social Security (Categorisation of Earners) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1978 (“the principal NI Regulations”) which provide, for the purposes of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 and the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992, for persons in employments of certain descriptions to be treated as falling within a different category of earners, and specify the person who is to be treated as the secondary contributor in respect of payments to certain description of earners. The amendments made by these Regulations mean the principal Regulations and the principal NI Regulations will no longer apply to lecturers, teachers etc employed at educational establishments.

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 2001 (S.I. 2001/1004) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Child Benefit (General) (Amendment) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend the Child Benefit (General) Regulations 2006 (“the General Regulations”) with effect from 6th April 2012.

items.title: The Social Security Benefits Up-rating Regulations 2012 (revoked)
items.description: This instrument contains only provisions in consequence of an order under sections 150 and 150A of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 (c.5). Accordingly, by virtue of section 172(1) and (3) of, and paragraph 3 of Schedule 7 to, that Act, the Secretary of State has not referred these Regulations to the Social Security Advisory Committee.

items.title: The A1(M) Motorway (Junction 36, Warmsworth) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Income Tax (Construction Industry Scheme) (Amendment) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Tax (Construction Industry Scheme) Regulations 2005 (“the 2005 Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security (Contributions) (Amendment No. 3) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 2001 (“the 2001 Regulations”).

items.title: The Income Tax (Pay As You Earn) (Amendment) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Tax (Pay As You Earn) Regulations 2003 (“the 2003 Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security (Industrial Injuries) (Dependency) (Permitted Earnings Limits) Order 2012
items.description: Paragraph 4 of Schedule 7 to the Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 increases the weekly rate of a disablement pension, where the beneficiary is entitled to child benefit in respect of one or more children or qualifying young persons.

items.title: The Social Security (Suspension of Payment of Benefits and Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend various sets of social security regulations.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 2003 (Commencement No. 27) Order 2012
items.description: This Order brings into force on 19th March 2012 section 29(1) to (3) and (5) and section 30 of the Criminal Justice Act 2003 for the purposes of allowing the following public prosecutors to institute criminal proceedings by issuing a written charge and requisition:

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Licences and Notices) (England) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations relate to licences issued in respect of English fishing boats, and fishing boats registered in the Channel Islands or the Isle of Man, under sections 4 and 4A of the Sea Fish (Conservation) Act 1967 (c.84), and to notices varying, suspending or revoking such licences. They revoke and replace, in relation to such licences and notices, the Sea Fishing (Licences and Notices) Regulations 1994 (S.I. 1994/2813).

items.title: The Licensing Act 2003 (Diamond Jubilee Licensing Hours) Order 2012
items.description: This Order is made under section 172 of the Licensing Act 2003, and marks the occasion of Her Majesty the Queen’s Diamond Jubilee in respect of which there is an extended Jubilee weekend for the period 2nd to 5th June 2012.

items.title: The Gambling (Operating Licence and Single-Machine Permit Fees) (Amendment) Regulations 2012 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Gambling (Operating Licence and Single-Machine Permit Fees) Regulations 2006 (“the 2006 Regulations”) made under the Gambling Act 2005 (“the Act”). The amendments create new fee categories and add to the list of ancillary remote operating licences. They set application and annual fees in respect of those categories and those licence types. They also correct a number of anomalies in the 2006 Regulations (as previously amended).

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Southend-on-Sea) Regulations 2012
The A30 Trunk Road (M25 Junction 13, Link Road) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The A1 Trunk Road (Astwick and Sandy, Central Bedfordshire) Northbound (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The A30 Trunk Road (Launceston to Okehampton) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Workmen’s Compensation (Supplementation) (Amendment) Scheme 2012
items.description: This Scheme amends the Workmen’s Compensation (Supplementation) Scheme 1982 (S.I.1982/1489) by making adjustments to the rate of lesser incapacity allowance. This is consequential upon the increase in the maximum rate of that allowance made by the Social Security Benefits Up-rating Order 2012 (S.I.2012/780).

items.title: The Guardian’s Allowance Up-rating Order 2012
items.description: This Order is made by the Treasury following a review of the general level of prices conducted under section 150 of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 (c. 5) in the tax year ending 5th April 2012.

items.title: The Guardian’s Allowance Up-rating (Northern Ireland) Order 2012
items.description: This Order is made by the Treasury following a review of the general level of prices conducted under section 150 of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 (c. 5) in the tax year ending 5th April 2012.

items.title: The Education (Student Loans) (Repayment) (Amendment) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Student Loans) (Repayment) Regulations 2009 (S.I. 2009/470) (“the Principal Regulations”). The Principal Regulations govern the repayment of income-contingent student loans paid to students under section 22 of the Teaching and Higher Education Act 1998.

items.title: The Policing of Aerodromes (Belfast International Airport) Order 2012
items.description: This Order provides that Belfast International Airport is not a “relevant aerodrome” for the purposes of Part 3 of the Aviation Security Act 1982 and accordingly does not require a police services agreement (specifying levels of policing and funding for policing by the aerodrome manager) and is not subject to the provisions of section 26(1) of the Act (which relate to the powers of constables and members of an aerodrome constabulary).

items.title: The Education (National Curriculum) (Key Stage 2 Assessment Arrangements) (England) (Amendment) Order 2012
items.description: This Order amends the Education (National Curriculum) (Key Stage 2 Assessment Arrangements) (England) Order 2003 (“the 2003 Order”) to include provisions about a National Curriculum test (“NC test”) in English writing, which will be administered to pupils in accordance with article 5 of the 2003 Order (which relates to NC tests as defined in article 3(1) of that Order).

items.title: The A1 Trunk Road (Elkesley Junctions Improvement) Order 2012
items.description: This order authorises the construction of two new two-way slip roads linking the A1 Trunk Road with side roads which the Secretary of State proposes to build under a separate Side Roads Order. The new slip roads will be trunk roads. Together, the new slip roads and the new side roads will constitute a grade-separated (two-level) junction the construction of which will enable the Secretary of State to close the existing A1 junctions at Jockey Lane / Cross Lane and High Street / Coalpit Lane.

items.title: The Education Act 2011 (Commencement No. 2 and Transitional and Savings Provisions) Order 2012
items.description: This Order brings into force on 15th January 2012 and 1st February 2012 certain provisions of the Education Act 2011 as listed in Articles 2 and 3 respectively.

items.title: The A663 Trunk Road (Burnley Lane Subway, Chadderton) (Temporary Prohibition of Pedestrians and Cyclists) Order 2012
The A1 Trunk Road (Thornhaugh, Peterborough) (Closure of Gap in the Central Reservation) Order 2012
The Apprenticeships (Form of Apprenticeship Agreement) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the form of the apprenticeship agreement for England and Wales under section 32 of that Act. Save where regulation 4 applies, an apprenticeship agreement must include the provisions contained in regulation 2.

items.title: The Guardian’s Allowance Up-rating Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations supersede the Guardian’s Allowance Up-rating Regulations 2011 (S.I. 2011/1039).

items.title: The Bus Lane Contraventions (Approved Local Authorities) (England) (Amendment) and Civil Enforcement of Parking Contraventions Designation Order 2012
items.description: This Order designates part of the area of each of The Derby City Council, The Hampshire County Council, The Northumberland County Council, The Solihull Metropolitan Borough Council and The Somerset County Council as a civil enforcement area for parking contraventions and as a special enforcement area for the purposes of Part 6 of the Traffic Management Act 2004.

items.title: The Data-gathering Powers (Relevant Data) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations specify the data (“relevant data”) that data-holders may be required to provide to an officer of Her Majesty’s Revenue and Customs under the provisions of Schedule 23 to the Finance Act 2011 (c. 11) (“Schedule 23”).

items.title: The Tax Credits (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2012
items.description: Starting on 6 April 2012

items.title: The Tax Credits Up-rating Regulations 2012
items.description: Section 41 of the Tax Credits Act 2002 (c. 21) requires the Treasury, in each tax year, to—

items.title: The A19 Trunk Road (Stockton Road Interchange to Portrack Interchange) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The M4 Motorway (Junctions 16-17) (Temporary Restriction of Traffic) Order 2012
The M18 Motorway (Junction 6) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Dunham Bridge (Revision of Tolls) Order 2012
The Jobseeker’s Allowance (Domestic Violence) (Amendment) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend the Jobseeker's Allowance Regulations 1996 (the JSA Regulations) to enable certain victims of domestic violence who are claiming a jobseeker's allowance to be treated as being available for and actively seeking employment, and as having a jobseeker's agreement (if they do not have one already). This will be the case for an initial period of 4 weeks and then, on production of relevant evidence, for a 13 week period, beginning on the date of the initial notification.

items.title: The Government Resources and Accounts Act 2000 (Audit of Public Bodies) Order 2012
items.description: The effect of the Order is to require the Comptroller and Auditor General to audit the accounts or financial statements of—

items.title: The A1(M) Motorway (Junction 42 to Junction 44) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The A50 Trunk Road (Sudbury - Derby, Derbyshire) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic in Laybys) Order 2012
The A50 Trunk Road (Uttoxeter - Derby) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic in Laybys) Order 2012
The A453 and A52 Trunk Roads (Nottingham) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The A46 Trunk Road (Aston Cross - Teddington Hands, Gloucestershire) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The M18 Motorway (Junction 1) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The M6 and M42 Motorways (M6 Junction 4 and M42 Junction 7a) and the A446 Trunk Road (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The M42 Motorway (Junction 5) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The M42 Motorway (Junction 6 to Junction 7) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Welfare Reform Act 2012 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2012
items.description: This Order is the first Commencement Order made under the Welfare Reform Act 2012 (“the Act”). It brings into force sections 47, 51 to 53, 58(1) and (2), 113 to 115, 128 to 133(1) to (4), 143, 144 and 146 of, and Part 14 of Schedule 14 to, the Act.

items.title: The A50 Trunk Road (Staffordshire) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic in Laybys) Order 2012
The M6 Motorway (Junctions 10 to 8) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The A5 Trunk Road (Mancetter Roundabout, Atherstone, Warwickshire) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) (No.2) Order 2012
The Social Security (Contributions) (Re-rating) Consequential Amendment Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 6th April 2012, amend the Social Security (Contributions) Regulations 2001 (“the principal Regulations”). They are made in consequence of the annual up-rating order under section 141 of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 and section 129 of the Social Security Administration (Northern Ireland) Act 1992.

items.title: The Business Premises Renovation Allowances (Amendment) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend the Business Premises Renovation Allowances Regulations 2007 (S.I. 2007/945) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Bournemouth) Regulations 2012
The A1(M) Motorway (Junction 59) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Shoreham-by-Sea) Regulations 2012
The London Thames Gateway Development Corporation (Transfer of Property, Rights and Liabilities) (Greater London Authority) Order 2012
items.description: This Order makes provision for the transfer to the Greater London Authority (“the GLA”) of property, rights and liabilities held by the London Thames Gateway Development Corporation (“the Corporation”).

items.title: The Energy Act 2011 (Commencement No 1 and Saving) Order 2012
items.description: This Order brings into force for all purposes the provisions of Chapter 1 of Part 1 of the Energy Act 2011 (which relates to the green deal), except for certain provisions which are brought into force for specified purposes only and certain provisions which are to be commenced by Scottish Ministers.

items.title: The Social Security (Lone Parents and Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Support (General) Regulations 1987 (“the Income Support Regulations”), the Jobseekers Allowance Regulations 1996 (“the JSA Regulations”), the Social Security (Work-focused Interviews for Lone Parents) and Miscellaneous Amendments Regulations 2000 (“the Lone Parents Regulations”), the Social Security (Jobcentre Plus Interviews) Regulations 2002 (“the Jobcentre Plus Regulations”) and the Employment and Support Allowance Regulations 2008 (“the ESA Regulations”).

items.title: The Staffordshire (Electoral Changes) Order 2012
items.description: This Order makes changes to the electoral arrangements for the county of Staffordshire following recommendations made by the Local Government Boundary Commission for England. The area of the county remains unchanged.

items.title: The A46 Trunk Road (Evesham Bypass) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Gloucestershire (Electoral Changes) Order 2012
items.description: This Order makes changes to the electoral arrangements for the county of Gloucestershire following recommendations made by the Local Government Boundary Commission for England. The area of the county remains unchanged.

items.title: The School Teachers’ Incentive Payments (England) Order 2012
items.description: This Order provides that lump sum payments paid to those school teachers in England who have attained 60 credits at masters level at the completion of the second year of the Mathematics Specialist Teacher Programme, which is funded by the Department for Education, are not to be treated as remuneration for the purpose of section 122(1) of the Education Act 2002.

items.title: The Flood and Water Management Act 2010 (Commencement No. 6 and Transitional Provisions) Order 2012
items.description: This Order brings into force provisions of the Flood and Water Management Act 2010 (“the Act”).

items.title: The A66 Trunk Road (Morton Palms Roundabout to Elton Interchange) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The M6 Motorway (Junction 7 to Junction 8) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Capital Gains Tax (Annual Exempt Amount) Order 2012
items.description: Section 3 of the Taxation of Chargeable Gains Act 1992 (“TCGA”) provides for an exemption from capital gains tax where an individual’s gains in any year of assessment are below a threshold which the Treasury are required to specify before that year. This Order provides that the annual exempt amount (“AEA”) for the assessment year 2012-13 is £11,200.00.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Consideration for Fuel Provided for Private Use) Order 2012
items.description: This order amends section 57 of the Value Added Tax Act 1994 (c.23) (“the Act”). References to sections 56 and 57 are references to sections 56 and 57 of the Act.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Increase of Registration Limits) Order 2012
items.description: This Order increases the VAT registration limits for taxable supplies and for acquisitions from other member States from £73,000 to £77,000, with effect from 1st April 2012.

items.title: The Registered Pension Schemes and Overseas Pension Schemes (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations make amendments relating to registered pension schemes and overseas pension schemes. Regulations 3 and 4 amend the Pension Schemes (Categories of Country and Requirements for Overseas Pension Schemes and Recognised Overseas Pension Schemes) Regulations 2006 (“Pension Regulations”). Those Regulations specify the requirements that an overseas pension scheme must satisfy.

items.title: The Landfill Tax (Amendment) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st April 2012, amend the Landfill Tax Regulations 1996 (S.I. 1996/1527) (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Child Trust Funds, Registered Pension Schemes and Stamp Duty Reserve Tax (Consequential Amendments) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations come into force on 6th April 2012 and amend the Child Trust Fund Regulations 2004 (S.I. 2004/1450), the Registered Pension Schemes (Discharge of Liabilities under Sections 267 and 268 of the Finance Act 2004) Regulations 2005 (S.I. 2005/3452) and the Stamp Duty Reserve Tax Regulations 1986 (S.I. 1986/1711).

items.title: The A1 Trunk Road (Carlton on Trent to Markham Moor, Nottinghamshire) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The M1 Motorway (Junction 21, Leicestershire) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The A1(M) Motorway (Junction 6, Northbound Exit Slip Road) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The M1 Motorway (Junctions 21 to 22) and the A46 Trunk Road (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Southport) Regulations 2012
The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Saltburn-by-the-Sea, Durham) Regulations 2012

The A27 Trunk Road (Oving Road) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The Air Navigation (Restriction of Flying) (Saltburn-by-the-Sea, Durham) (Revocation) Regulations 2012
The Police Reform and Social Responsibility Act 2011 (Commencement No. 4) Order 2012
items.description: This Order brings the following provisions of the Police Reform and Social Responsibility Act 2011 into force on 22nd March 2012: sections 105 to 108 (partially, in each case), sections 113 to 115 (partially, in each case) and section 118 (partially). These provisions are brought into force for the purposes of making regulations under, or in accordance with, the provision which they each insert into the Licensing Act 2003.

items.title: The Qualifications for Appointment of Members to the First-tier Tribunal and Upper Tribunal (Amendment) Order 2012
items.description: This Order amends the Qualifications for Appointment of Members to the First-tier Tribunal and Upper Tribunal Order 2008. It adds as one of the grounds of eligibility for appointment as a member of the First-tier Tribunal or the Upper Tribunal who is not a judge, substantial experience in environmental matters.

items.title: The Environmental Protection Act 1990 (Commencement No. 19) Order 2012
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st April 2012 the repeal of section 1 of the Refuse Disposal (Amenity) Act 1978 (c. 3) in England and Wales.

items.title: The School Admissions (Appeals Arrangements) (England) Regulations 2012
items.description: These Regulations prescribe matters relating to appeals brought under sections 94 and 95 of the School Standards and Framework Act 1998, as amended by the Education Act 2002, the Education and Inspections Act 2006, and the Education and Skills Act 2008. They prescribe the manner in which an appeal panel is to be constituted (regulation 5 and the Schedule) and the payment of allowances to appeal panel members by the body or bodies responsible for making the arrangements in respect of financial loss, and travel and subsistence expenses (regulation 6).

items.title: The M11 Motorway (Junction 6, Southbound Link Road) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The M25 Motorway and the A282 Trunk Road (Junctions 30 - 31) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) (No. 2) Order 2012
The National Health Service Trust Development Authority (Establishment and Constitution) Order 2012 (revoked)
items.description: This Order provides for the establishment and constitution of a Special Health Authority under section 28 of the National Health Service Act 2006, to be known as the National Health Service Trust Development Authority (“the Authority”), to exercise such of the Secretary of State’s functions in connection with the performance management and development of National Health Service trusts in England, and the making of certain public appointments to NHS bodies in England and of their trustees, and such other functions as the Secretary of State may direct.

items.title: The M20 Motorway (Junctions 8 - 7, Londonbound) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The M25 Motorway (Junction 18, Slip Roads) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
The M4 Motorway (Junctions 1 - 4A) (Temporary Restriction and Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2012
